title: File browser + export dialog + keyboard shortcuts
analysis:
Task 97 requires 4 main features: (1) File browser sidebar with tree view of .ri files, (2) Export dialog with format selector + native save dialog + progress/toast, (3) Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+O, F5, Ctrl+E), (4) File-changed event detection with reload prompt.

Existing codebase has:
- bridge.ts with exportGeometry(entityPath, outputPath) — NOTE: signature doesn't match backend export(format, path) command. The bridge invokes 'export_geometry' but the backend registers 'export'. Parameter names also mismatch (entityPath vs format). Need to fix.
- bridge.ts onFileChanged(callback) already wired to 'file-changed' backend event with FileData payload
- editorStore with openFile/closeFile/setActiveFile/markDirty/markClean
- App.tsx with handleExport() as a stub comment, handleFitToView() stub
- All panel components follow pattern: Component with exported Props interface, CSS module, data-testid, export from panels/index.ts
- Tests use vitest + @solidjs/testing-library, mock @tauri-apps/api/core and @tauri-apps/api/event
- Backend export command takes format ('step'|'stl') and path strings
- Backend does NOT have a list_project_files command — FileBrowser must be prop-driven
- Editor.tsx already handles Ctrl+S for save via CodeMirror keymap
- App.test.tsx mocks bridge module, Viewport, Editor, and FileTabs — new components may need similar mocks
- No hooks/ directory exists yet — will be created for useKeyboardShortcuts

Review feedback (blocking): ExportDialog Export button lacks disabled={props.exporting}, allowing concurrent export clicks during the async export window. Cancel button should also be disabled during export to prevent closing the dialog mid-export without seeing the result.

prerequisites.description: Fix bridge.ts exportGeometry: change signature from (entityPath, outputPath) to (format, outputPath), update invoke call from 'export_geometry' to 'export' with {format, path: outputPath} params to match the backend command handler. This is a breaking change but handleExport() in App.tsx is currently a stub so nothing depends on the old signature.

Add ExportFormat type to types.ts: type ExportFormat = 'step' | 'stl' | '3mf'. Add FileEntry interface for file browser tree: { path: string; name: string; isDirectory: boolean; children?: FileEntry[] }. Add ToastMessage interface: { id: string; type: 'success' | 'error' | 'info'; message: string }.
Create gui/src/hooks/ directory for reusable hook functions (useKeyboardShortcuts).

steps.description: Write failing tests for Toast component in __tests__/Toast.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='toast', (2) displays message text from props, (3) applies correct data-type attribute for 'success' and 'error' types, (4) renders a close button, (5) clicking close button calls onDismiss callback, (6) hidden when message is null/empty.

Implement Toast component in panels/Toast.tsx + Toast.module.css. Props: { message: string; type: 'success' | 'error' | 'info'; onDismiss: () => void }. Uses data-testid='toast', data-type attribute for styling, close button. Add export to panels/index.ts.

Write failing tests for FileBrowser in __tests__/FileBrowser.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='file-browser', (2) renders a list item for each file in files prop, (3) displays basename of file path (not full path), (4) the item matching activeFile prop has data-active='true' attribute, (5) clicking a file item calls onFileClick(path), (6) shows 'No files' empty state when files array is empty.

Implement FileBrowser component in panels/FileBrowser.tsx + FileBrowser.module.css. Props: { files: FileData[]; activeFile: string | null; onFileClick: (path: string) => void }. Uses For to iterate files, Show for empty state. Each entry shows basename, has data-active attribute when matching activeFile, click calls onFileClick. Add export to panels/index.ts.

Write failing tests for ExportDialog in __tests__/ExportDialog.test.tsx: (1) renders modal with data-testid='export-dialog' when open=true, (2) not in DOM when open=false, (3) format selector has STEP/STL/3MF options, (4) default selected format is 'step', (5) clicking Export button calls onExport(selectedFormat), (6) clicking Cancel calls onClose, (7) changing format selector updates selection, (8) shows progress indicator when exporting=true prop set.

Implement ExportDialog component in panels/ExportDialog.tsx + ExportDialog.module.css. Props: { open: boolean; exporting: boolean; onExport: (format: ExportFormat) => void; onClose: () => void }. Modal overlay with format <select>, Export/Cancel buttons, progress indicator. Uses Show for conditional rendering. Add export to panels/index.ts.

Write failing tests for useKeyboardShortcuts in __tests__/useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts: (1) dispatching Ctrl+O keydown on document calls onOpen callback, (2) dispatching F5 keydown calls onReEvaluate, (3) dispatching Ctrl+E keydown calls onExportDialog, (4) dispatching Ctrl+O when target is an <input> does NOT call onOpen (avoids conflict), (5) after cleanup, dispatching shortcuts does nothing (listeners removed).

Implement useKeyboardShortcuts hook in hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts. Accepts callbacks: { onOpen?: () => void; onReEvaluate?: () => void; onExportDialog?: () => void }. Registers document keydown listener on mount (via SolidJS onMount), removes on cleanup (onCleanup). Maps Ctrl+O, F5, Ctrl+E. Skips if event target is input/textarea/[contenteditable].

Write failing tests for ReloadPrompt in __tests__/ReloadPrompt.test.tsx: (1) renders banner with data-testid='reload-prompt' when filePath prop is non-null, (2) not in DOM when filePath is null, (3) displays the basename of filePath in the message, (4) clicking Reload button calls onReload, (5) clicking Dismiss button calls onDismiss.

Implement ReloadPrompt component in panels/ReloadPrompt.tsx + ReloadPrompt.module.css. Props: { filePath: string | null; onReload: () => void; onDismiss: () => void }. Thin banner above editor: '{basename} changed on disk. Reload?' with Reload and Dismiss buttons. Uses Show for conditional rendering. Add export to panels/index.ts.

Update panels-smoke.test.tsx to include all new components: add FileBrowser (with files=[] and onFileClick=vi.fn()), ExportDialog (with open=true, exporting=false, onExport=vi.fn(), onClose=vi.fn()), Toast (with message/type/onDismiss), ReloadPrompt (with filePath/onReload/onDismiss). Verify all data-testid attributes present.

Wire all new components into App.tsx and update layout: (1) Add FileBrowser in editor panel showing editorStore.state.openFiles, wire onFileClick to setActiveFile, (2) Add ExportDialog state (showExportDialog signal), wire handleExport to open it, wire onExport to call bridge.exportGeometry with format + native save dialog path (mock-safe), show Toast on success/error, (3) Wire useKeyboardShortcuts: Ctrl+O opens file via bridge.openFile, F5 re-evaluates by calling updateSource, Ctrl+E toggles export dialog, (4) Subscribe to bridge.onFileChanged in onMount, track changedFile signal, show ReloadPrompt for open files, wire Reload to update content, Dismiss to clear prompt, (5) Update App.module.css with fileBrowser class for layout, (6) Update App.test.tsx bridge mock to include onFileChanged. Ensure all new components render in App smoke tests.

Add failing tests to __tests__/ExportDialog.test.tsx for concurrent-invocation guard: (1) 'Export button is disabled when exporting=true' — render with exporting=true, query the Export button, assert it has disabled attribute and clicking it does NOT call onExport. (2) 'Cancel button is disabled when exporting=true' — render with exporting=true, query the Cancel button, assert it has disabled attribute and clicking it does NOT call onClose. (3) 'Export button is enabled when exporting=false' — render with exporting=false, assert Export button does NOT have disabled attribute. (4) 'format selector is disabled when exporting=true' — render with exporting=true, assert the <select> has disabled attribute to prevent changing format mid-export.

Fix ExportDialog.tsx to guard against concurrent invocation: (1) Add disabled={props.exporting} to the Export button (line 48). (2) Add disabled={props.exporting} to the Cancel button (line 42) to prevent closing the dialog mid-export without seeing the result. (3) Add disabled={props.exporting} to the format <select> (line 29) to prevent changing format during an in-progress export. All three are gated on the existing props.exporting boolean which App.tsx already manages.

design_decisions.decision: FileBrowser is a prop-driven presentational component receiving FileData[]
design_decisions.rationale: No backend list_files/list_project_files command exists. Making it prop-driven keeps the component testable and decoupled. App.tsx derives file list from editorStore.state.openFiles. The component interface is future-proof — when a backend file listing command is added, only the data source in App changes, not the component.

design_decisions.decision: Fix bridge.ts exportGeometry to match backend 'export' command
design_decisions.rationale: Current bridge invokes 'export_geometry' with {entityPath, outputPath} but backend registers command as 'export' with {format, path}. This is a latent bug — export can never work with current signature. Fixing as prerequisite since ExportDialog depends on it. No existing callers break because handleExport in App.tsx is a stub.

design_decisions.decision: Toast as standalone reusable component in panels/
design_decisions.rationale: Toast notifications are needed by export dialog (success/failure) and potentially other features. Following existing pattern of putting UI components in panels/ directory with CSS modules and data-testid.

design_decisions.decision: ReloadPrompt as separate component rather than inline in Editor
design_decisions.rationale: Editor.tsx is already complex with CodeMirror integration. ReloadPrompt is a thin banner that App.tsx controls based on file-changed events from bridge.onFileChanged. Separation follows single-responsibility and makes testing straightforward.

design_decisions.decision: Keyboard shortcuts skip when input/textarea/contenteditable is focused
design_decisions.rationale: Ctrl+O and Ctrl+E would conflict with typing in PropertyEditor inputs. F5 is safe but consistent behavior is better. CodeMirror handles its own keybindings internally (Ctrl+S already works), so we only check for plain HTML inputs. The hook checks event.target tagName.

design_decisions.decision: ExportDialog uses controlled props (open/exporting) rather than internal state
design_decisions.rationale: Following Solid.js controlled component pattern consistent with how PropertyEditor accepts values as props. App.tsx manages dialog open state, wiring it to both Toolbar Export button and Ctrl+E shortcut. This makes testing straightforward — just set props.

Keyboard shortcuts are cross-cutting logic with lifecycle (mount/cleanup) but no reactive state store. A hooks/ directory follows common Solid/React conventions for this pattern. Keeps App.tsx clean and the logic unit-testable in isolation.

design_decisions.decision: Export dialog handles native save dialog in App.tsx wiring, not inside ExportDialog component
design_decisions.rationale: ExportDialog is a pure UI component that emits onExport(format). App.tsx handles the async flow: open native dialog (via @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog), get path, call bridge.exportGeometry(format, path), show toast. This keeps ExportDialog testable without mocking Tauri plugins.

design_decisions.decision: Disable Export, Cancel, and format selector when exporting=true
design_decisions.rationale: Reviewer identified that between the user clicking Export and the dialog closing (in handleDoExport's finally block in App.tsx), there is an unbounded async window during which the user can click Export again, firing concurrent bridgeExportGeometry calls. For large models this window can be several seconds. Disabling all interactive controls (not just Export) prevents changing format mid-export and prevents closing the dialog without seeing the result. The exporting prop already exists and is managed by App.tsx, so no new state is needed.

reuse.where: All existing components in panels/ (Toolbar, StatusBar, PropertyEditor, ConstraintPanel)
reuse.how: New components follow same pattern: import styles from './Component.module.css', use styles.className for styling, consistent variable naming from theme.ts

reuse.where: All existing components (toolbar, status-bar, property-editor, constraint-panel, editor-container, file-tabs, etc.)
All tests in __tests__/ (Toolbar.test.tsx, ConstraintPanel.test.tsx, FileTabs.test.tsx, etc.)
reuse.how: New tests follow same structure: describe blocks, render(() => <Component />), screen.getByTestId/getByText, fireEvent for interactions, vi.fn() for callbacks

reuse.what: Tauri API mocking pattern (vi.mock before imports)
reuse.how: Tests needing bridge/Tauri mocks use vi.mock('@tauri-apps/api/core'), vi.mock('@tauri-apps/api/event'). ExportDialog app integration can mock @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog similarly.
ExportDialog flow calls bridge.exportGeometry(format, outputPath) after user selects format and save path
FileBrowser onFileClick uses bridge.openFile to load content, then editorStore.openFile to add to tabs. ReloadPrompt checks editorStore.state.openFiles.

reuse.where: bridge.ts line 116-122, already defined and typed with FileData payload
reuse.how: App.tsx subscribes in onMount (alongside existing engineStore.subscribeToEvents). When event fires for an open file, shows ReloadPrompt.

reuse.where: panels/index.ts currently exports PropertyEditor, ConstraintPanel, Toolbar, StatusBar
reuse.how: Add exports for FileBrowser, ExportDialog, Toast, ReloadPrompt following same pattern

reuse.where: All panel components define exported Props interface (ToolbarProps, StatusBarProps, etc.)
reuse.how: New components export FileBrowserProps, ExportDialogProps, ToastProps, ReloadPromptProps

reuse.what: For/Show SolidJS primitives for conditional and list rendering
reuse.where: ConstraintPanel.tsx (For + Show), PropertyEditor.tsx (For + Show), FileTabs.tsx (For + Show)
reuse.how: FileBrowser uses For for file list + Show for empty state. ExportDialog uses Show for open/close. Toast uses Show for visibility. ReloadPrompt uses Show for filePath check.

reuse.where: ExportDialog.tsx line 7 (ExportDialogProps.exporting), App.tsx manages the signal
reuse.how: The exporting prop already exists and is used for the progress indicator. Steps 13-14 reuse it to also disable interactive controls, requiring zero new state or prop changes.
